title: Fix suppressing errors on unreachable (by parameter value) code
body:
I have some code that looks something like this:

    if (AXIS_PCIE_DATA_WIDTH == 64) begin
        // snip
    end else begin
        // snip
        m_axis_cc_tdata_int[AXIS_PCIE_DATA_WIDTH-1:96] = shift_axi_rdata[AXIS_PCIE_DATA_WIDTH-1:96];
    end

(whole file at https://github.com/alexforencich/verilog-pcie/blob/master/rtl/pcie_us_axi_dma_rd.v)

where `AXIS_PCIE_DATA_WIDTH` is a module parameter.  When `AXIS_PCIE_DATA_WIDTH` is set to 64, verilator reports errors:

    %Error: ../../rtl/pcie_us_axi_master_rd.v:761:44: [63:96] Range extract has backward bit ordering, perhaps you wanted [96:63]
                                                    : ... In instance pcie_us_axi_master.pcie_us_axi_master_rd_inst
      761 |                         m_axis_cc_tdata_int[AXIS_PCIE_DATA_WIDTH-1:96] = shift_axi_rdata[AXIS_PCIE_DATA_WIDTH-1:96];
          |                                            ^
    %Error: ../../rtl/pcie_us_axi_master_rd.v:761:89: [63:96] Range extract has backward bit ordering, perhaps you wanted [96:63]
                                                    : ... In instance pcie_us_axi_master.pcie_us_axi_master_rd_inst
      761 |                         m_axis_cc_tdata_int[AXIS_PCIE_DATA_WIDTH-1:96] = shift_axi_rdata[AXIS_PCIE_DATA_WIDTH-1:96];
          |   

However, this code is unreachable with that parameter value, so this should not be an error.  The same module also has cases that trigger "extracting X bits from only Y bit number" from similarly unreachable lines.
